Name: A Time To Kill / 殺戮時刻
Notes: For a movie released in the 90s, maybe it was nice. Today it was still a feel-good movie for the white educated. Maybe it did pictured the way it was but most characters were plain and only supportive. Like, ok we get it Mathew is hot and he got two hot women who both liked him and he in the end did the right thing and retreated seduction, and we want to be him, right?

Name: The Quick and The Dead
Director: Featuring Sharon Stone, Gene Hackman, Leonardo Dicaprio, Russel Crowe
Notes:
A modern twist on traditional western: I see this movie as an epitome of feminism: LOTS of feminist analysis could be found here: ways of revenge, anxiety, despair, self-doubt, creativity, destroy, reconstruct, sexual desire and freedom. Also very interesting that the girl shot her own father dead in the beginning - could this be a male perspective’s redemption?  
In fact, not just female, you can see native american and black american roles, challenging white male supremacy.
